frameworks.name: beta-VAE disentangled generative model
frameworks.one_line: unsupervised deep generative model factorising sensory data into interpretable latent factors

frameworks.name: deep supervised classifier model of ventral stream
frameworks.one_line: high-dimensional distributed population coding view of IT responses

frameworks.name: single-neuron semantic coding
frameworks.one_line: individual IT cells map onto interpretable disentangled generative factors

core_question: Do single inferotemporal face-patch neurons encode interpretable disentangled generative factors rather than only a distributed high-dimensional code?
key_assumptions: beta-VAE latents approximate the disentangling objective of the ventral stream
single-unit IT responses can be meaningfully compared to model latent factors
face stimuli admit a low-dimensional semantically interpretable factorisation
manipulations: modelling IT face-patch responses with an unsupervised beta-VAE versus a supervised classifier
varying generative latent factors (e.g. gender, hair length)
reconstructing face images from population subsets of varying size
key_findings: beta-VAE latent factors correspond closely to factors coded by single IT neurons
face images can be reconstructed from signals of just a handful of cells
IT face code is more interpretable at the single-unit level than classifier models predict
interpretations: the ventral stream may optimise a disentangling objective
the resulting neural code is low-dimensional and semantically interpretable at the single-unit level
